frameworks.name: bump-attractor / diffusing-particle working memory
frameworks.one_line: Memory of spatial angles modeled as Brownian-diffusing particles whose variance grows linearly with delay.

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / decision variable formation
frameworks.one_line: Continuous decision variable computed and stored under capacity- and time-limited working memory.

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: Decision precision quantified via error variance as function of set size and delay.

core_question: How do capacity and temporal limitations of working memory constrain the fidelity of a continuously valued decision variable formed by averaging multiple visual locations?
key_assumptions: Memory of a spatial angle behaves as a diffusing particle with static plus time-dependent noise
Diffusion cost of holding N items follows sigma_N^2 = sigma_1^2 * N^A
Participants adopt one of two storage strategies (Average-then-Diffuse or Diffuse-then-Average)
manipulations: Set size of remembered disks (1, 2, or 5)
Delay between presentation and report (0, 1, or 6 s)
Task type (Perceived single-disk vs Computed mean angle)
Temporal structure (Simultaneous vs Sequential presentation of disks)
key_findings: Error variance for both Perceived and Computed reports grew linearly with delay
Computed-mean precision degraded with set size and delay, but degradation magnitude depended on strategy
Individual participants were best fit by either an AtD or a DtA storage strategy
Sequential presentation required mixture-of-strategies extensions, indicating within-trial integration of late evidence
interpretations: Decision variables are not stored uniformly: strategy choice (single-summary vs multi-item) governs how working-memory limits propagate to decision accuracy
Future neural and computational models of deliberative decision-making must incorporate strategy- and task-dependent working-memory constraints
limitations_noted: Two-model framework may not exhaust the space of plausible storage strategies, with mixtures needed for Sequential blocks
